summary: R4 resolves the R3 systems_architect blocker. The browser-visible map remains MapRenderer/Pixi-owned, React owns only DOM controls and UI intent, and the R4 lifecycle/sizing fix makes the Pixi surface deterministic under the prior repeat Chromium reproduction. MapRenderer waits for a renderable host, initializes Pixi with explicit WebGL dimensions, supports abortable stale mounts, and reports controlled mount status/error state outside the Pixi-owned host. Diff scope remains within allowed paths, and I found no hidden sim-core rule implementation, save/schema change, Electron IPC expansion, RNG/time authority, full WorldState import into React/Redux, or new production dependency.

tests_run.result: On feat/m2-client-map. Modified and untracked files are within the task's allowed implementation, test, task, and message/route paths; no forbidden paths were touched.
R4 writer handoff JSON is valid.
1 test file passed; 2 tests passed, including render-plan modes and replace-read-model presentation-cache rebuild.
format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed. Architecture self-test produced 15 expected violations.
Vite production build succeeded. Output still includes broad Pixi runtime chunks, matching the recorded non-blocking risk.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic hello, command/query, and save/load hashes matched; root Vitest passed 19 files/89 tests; web Vitest passed 1 file/3 tests.

tests_run.result: Prior R3 failure reproduction passed: 6 Chromium tests passed, with repeated page loads reaching renderer-mounted state.

Normal Chromium Playwright run passed: 2 tests passed, covering map load, zoom, district and settlement selection, seasonal/economy/routes mode switching, route previews, selected details, and renderer mount state.

decisions: ACCEPT: R4 satisfies the task acceptance criteria and resolves the R3 request-changes item for deterministic browser-visible Pixi mounting.

The visible map is now acceptable for M2 as a MapRenderer/Pixi-owned surface. React passes snapshot, viewport, selection callback, and abort signal, but does not manage per-map-entity DOM/SVG or Pixi DisplayObjects.

Map presentation instructions, including district fills, seasonal/economy/routes palette choices, route stroke/width, labels, selection visuals, hit handling, and read-model rebuild/applyDelta semantics, remain in packages/map-renderer.

The protocol-shaped M2 fixture is acceptable as a non-blocking M2 client risk because live worker query integration is explicitly outside this task and no full WorldState is imported into React/Redux.
No new ADR is needed for R4; the change implements the existing frozen React/Pixi boundary and WebGL2 Pixi baseline rather than changing architecture.
risks: The M2 web map still uses a protocol-shaped client fixture until live worker query integration is scheduled.

pixi.js top-level runtime still emits broad chunks, including WebGPU-related chunks, even though MapRenderer initializes with WebGL preference. A narrower Pixi bundle remains a future package/dependency review topic.
The pre-existing Vite warning for apps/web/src/worker/hello-simulation-adapter.mjs dynamic import remains visible during e2e and was not changed by this task.
requested_action: Lead should validate this ACCEPT handoff, run any integration-level gates required by the task workflow, and decide task acceptance/integration. Do not mark the task accepted solely from this reviewer handoff without lead validation.
